search_enabled_cot.prompt: Ganga, a Hindu river goddess, described in mythology as a celestial being who descended from heaven to Earth, associated with purity, salvation, and life-giving water. She is often depicted as a beautiful woman wearing a white sari, riding a makara (a mythical aquatic creature), and holding a water vessel (kalash).
